steps.title: Spot the decisive contrast
steps.content: The word though sets up a contrast between how extensively high altitude studies have been conducted and the notice that below sea level studies have received. The blank should describe the state of high altitude research that opposes “comparatively little notice.”

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Abundant directly means plentiful—the opposite of “little notice” for below sea level research. This creates the contrast the sentence needs: high altitude studies are extensive while below sea level studies remain understudied.

Equivocal, sporadic, and preliminary describe the quality of findings, their scattered occurrence, or their incomplete status—none of which create the quantity/neglect contrast.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
